stories.title: Skubal Targeting June 12 Return at Cleveland — The Ace Is Almost Back

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal is targeting a June 12 return at Cleveland — just five days from now.

After a dominant rehab start (5 IP, 0 ER, 6 K, 54 pitches), the Tigers' ace is almost back.

The two-time Cy Young winner could start as soon as Friday. #Tigers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
If Skubal is back June 12 and pitching well, every MLB contender shifts its trade deadline strategy.

He's a two-time Cy Young. Free agent after 2026. Tigers are 6-21 without him.

ESPN's Jeff Passan says the market is already loaded. #Tigers #MLB #TradeDeadline

article.headline: Tarik Skubal Is Targeting June 12 at Cleveland — The Tigers' Ace Is Almost Back

research.facts:
Skubal Targeting June 12 Return
**Sources:**
- Detroit News: "Tarik Skubal provides update on when he'll return to Tigers rotation" (June 8, 2026)
- Yardbarker: "Tarik Skubal Return Timeline: Tigers Star Drops Realistic Response After Surgery"
- Total Pro Sports: "Tarik Skubal Reveals Realistic Return Timeline After Surgery"
- MLB.com: "Tarik Skubal makes rehab start at High-A West Michigan"
- Detroit Sports Nation: "June Return in Play After Tarik Skubal Elbow Surgery"
- ESPN: Passan's trade deadline intel piece (all 30 teams)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Skubal had arthroscopic left elbow surgery May 6, 2026
- Rehab start June 7 (High-A West Michigan): 5 IP, 0 ER, 2H, 6K, 0BB, 54 pitches (44 strikes)
- Detroit News reports June 12 at Cleveland as the target return date
- Manager A.J. Hinch considering one more rehab start vs. direct activation
- Skubal won Cy Young in back-to-back seasons (two-time winner)
- Tigers record in his starts vs. without him in 2026: 6-21 without him
- Skubal is a free agent after the 2026 season
**Freshness:** Rehab start June 7, return target reported June 8. Follow-up story with June 12 target confirmed.

stories.title: Giants 2, Cubs 1 (10 Innings) — Sunday Night Baseball Recap

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Matt Chapman's 10th-inning RBI single lifted the Giants over the Cubs 2-1 on Sunday Night Baseball.

San Francisco won their fourth game in five — strong pitching held Chicago all night.

The NL Central landscape shifts. #MLB #Giants #Cubs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Chicago struggled on Sunday Night Baseball — 2-1 loss to San Francisco in extras.

The Cubs are still in the postseason mix but can't afford to give ground in June.

Braves 45-21. The NL keeps moving. #MLB #Cubs

- Strong pitching held Chicago offense all game
- Giants scored game winner via automatic runner rule in extras

stories.title: Schwarber Paces the MLB Home Run Race Alone

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB in home runs — alone now that Judge and Murakami are on the IL.

He hit No. 20 on May 15, the earliest that milestone has ever been reached in MLB history.

On pace for 65+ HRs. The 60-HR club: Bonds, McGwire, Sosa. That's it. #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Paces the HR Race Alone: On Pace for 65+ with Judge and Murakami on the IL

research.facts:
Schwarber HR Race
**Sources:**
- ESPN: "MLB 2026: Will Phillies' Kyle Schwarber join 60 home run club?"
- Heavy.com: "Kyle Schwarber's Home Run Pace Rivals Barry Bonds"
- ClutchPoints: "Kyle Schwarber joins elite Phillies club with 20th home run of 2026"
- Fox News/OutKick: "Philadelphia Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber is already on track to challenge Barry Bonds, Mark McGwire"
- SI/Phillies: "Kyle Schwarber Still On Franchise Record Home Run Pace"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Schwarber hit his 20th HR on May 15 — the earliest date in MLB history
- On pace for 65-70 HRs based on current trajectory
- Aaron Judge on IL (rib stress fracture)
- Roki Murakami on IL (hamstring)
- Schwarber is sole MLB HR leader
- 60-HR club members: Barry Bonds (73, 2001), Mark McGwire (70, 1998; 65, 1999), Sammy Sosa (66, 1998; 64, 2001; 63, 1999)
- Last NL player to hit 60+ HRs: Sosa (64) in 2001
**Confidence:** HIGH on all core facts from multiple sources.

stories.title: Yankees Navigating Judge's Absence — Jones Recalled, Rice Contributing

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge is out 4-6 weeks with a rib stress fracture.

Spencer Jones was recalled from Triple-A to fill the void. Ben Rice is still raking.

The Yankees are 37-26 — holding pace with the Rays in the AL East without their MVP. #Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Yankees Adapting Without Judge: Spencer Jones Recalled, Ben Rice Producing as New York Holds Pace

Yankees Without Judge — Jones and Rice
**Sources:**
- US News: "Yankees Put Aaron Judge on Injured List With Rib Stress Fracture, Recall Spencer Jones From Triple-A"
- Washington Post: "Yankees put Aaron Judge on injured list with rib stress fracture, recall Spencer Jones from Triple-A"
- SI/OnSI: "Aaron Judge Injury Update: Can Spencer Jones Fill The Void For The Yankees?"
- Yahoo Sports: "Aaron Judge Injury Update: Can Spencer Jones Fill The Void For The Yankees?"
- Athlon Sports: "Yankees Recall Spencer Jones Amid Aaron Judge Injury Update"
- Yardbarker: "Yankees to recall Spencer Jones after Aaron Judge injury"
- ESPN: "Time to panic in the Bronx? What Aaron Judge's injury means for Yankees, the AL East and the trade deadline"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Aaron Judge placed on IL with rib stress fracture (stress fracture in upper right rib)
- Injury timeline: 4-6 weeks before reevaluation
- Spencer Jones (25) recalled from Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre
- Jones limited MLB track record: .167/.259/.167 slashline in prior call-up
- Ben Rice has been contributing for the Yankees
- Yankees record: 37-26 (one game behind Rays 37-25 in AL East)
- Judge return estimated: late July, around the August 3 trade deadline
**Note:** Judge injury was June 5 announcement; June 9 is follow-up on how Yankees are managing.

stories.title: The Braves Are Pulling Away — Baseball's Best Team at 45-21

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Atlanta Braves own baseball's best record at 45-21 — with a 9.5-game lead in the NL East.

Chris Sale leads an elite rotation. Run differential: +109.

While the NL West stays crowded, Atlanta is quietly pulling away. #Braves #MLB
